reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome BEFORE building — the real ONNX graph turned photos into sparse line drawings whose ink lands on scene structure, not noise; then the full demo was driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver across all 5 pages; desktop+mobile 0px overflow measured), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21, WASM, fresh profile) BEFORE building. The real Informative Drawings ONNX graph (rocca/informative-drawings-line-art-onnx, single-file model.onnx ~17 MB) was loaded via ort.InferenceSession.create. inputNames=['input'], outputNames=['output']; output dims [1,1,H,W] float32 in [0,1]. On a city-street photo the result was a genuine sparse line drawing — 77% white paper, ~22% line/mid-tone structure grounded in the scene's actual edges — and on a non-square 512x288 mountain photo ~40% structure. NOT noise, NOT a flat field, NOT identity. Then the deployed demo ran end-to-end: Trace produced a real drawing at inference 2739 ms + composite 4 ms.
rubric.notes: Inference AND the dense composite (grayscale drawing + indigo ink-overlay) run off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the line drawing + overlay ImageBitmaps are transferred (not cloned) back.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a single-channel line map [1,1,H,W] in [0,1] (~1=paper, low=ink), same spatial size as the (multiple-of-8-rounded) input. The worker renders it as a grayscale drawing (gray = value·255), an accent-tinted overlay (indigo painted where 1−value is high, over the dimmed photo), and an ink-coverage fraction (pixels below the ink threshold). Semantic sanity: ink coverage tracks scene structure (a clean portrait inked 6.2%, denser scenes more) and the overlay's lines land on real boundaries — evidence of a learned drawing, not an edge filter.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT capability vs every built vision demo: photo -> artistic line drawing (line/soft-edge extraction). Not built before; distinct from the built restoration/depth/segmentation/detection demos and from a Sobel/Canny edge map.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (rocca/informative-drawings-line-art-onnx, linked) AND the upstream (carolineec/informative-drawings, MIT, linked), the task (image-to-image / photo->line drawing), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 — noting transformers.js has no informative-drawings class/task), the real I/O tensor names + shapes (input [1,3,H,W] / output [1,1,H,W]), quantisation (fp32 single file), backend (WASM), download (~17 MB) and license (upstream MIT; the ONNX re-export restates none — disclosed honestly). No invented API surface.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only (the isolated per-worker ORT-web escape hatch, like nafnet/iat/lama/microdehaze) — shared lib/webai.js is untouched.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'rocca/informative-drawings-line-art-onnx', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:17}). The model is fetched THROUGH Cache Storage (cache name informative-drawings-onnx-cache) so lib/model-cache.js scans '/rocca/informative-drawings-line-art-onnx/' and a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~17 MB). Verified in headless Chrome: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it downloaded and flipped the loader to ready. A per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: Never silently re-downloads; the ~17 MB blob is streamed with progress.

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error states are announced in role=status aria-live regions (shared loader + page #status). Download progress streams a percent. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Tracing failed: …'); the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere (conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds).
rubric.notes: Measured 0 console errors and 0 network failures on load AND run across all 5 pages.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the accessible before/after reveal range (aria-valuetext '% line drawing'), the sample gallery (rights-safe thumbnails as labelled buttons), the upload/drop dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space, drag+drop), the Trace button, and a Download-drawing anchor. Ladder pages add: practical's detail/resolution slider (384-1024px); wild's ink-coverage leaderboard with a Clear button; multi-model's Depth Anything chain.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built nafnet/iat/microdehaze image-to-image demos; the reveal wipe uses a native range input for full keyboard support.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real ONNX graph and the displayed drawing, overlay and ink-coverage number are all derived from the real 'output' tensor. There is no synthetic pre-degradation at all (unlike deblur/dehaze which add a known degradation to demo the fix); this is a straight photo->drawing conversion, so what you see is exactly the model's output.

All canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-labels (source photo / line drawing / ink overlay); every button has an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the loader status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext. Light+dark inherit the WCAG-AA design system. Gallery thumbnails are real buttons with aria-pressed + license attribution on selection.
rubric.notes: Consulted modern-web-guidance for accessible live-region status + exposing canvas content; applied.

rubric.evidence: Mobile+desktop MATRIX measured 0 px horizontal overflow on ALL 5 pages at mobile (360×740, DPR3) AND desktop (1280×800). The two-col layout is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; the param-grid is auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); reveal/canvases are inline-size:100%; the leaderboard table + readout wrap. scripts/responsive-check on the index reported desktop clean · mobile clean.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Mobile download cost (~17 MB fp32, plus ~100 MB Depth Anything on the multi-model page) surfaced in at-a-glance / the page copy.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense-output composite (line map -> grayscale drawing + indigo overlay, per-pixel) run in the Web Worker; the drawing + overlay ImageBitmaps are transferred, not cloned (invariant 15's dense-output-off-main-thread rule). The main thread only draws bounded fit-canvases + the reveal composite. A 512-px pass is ~2.7 s on single-thread WASM; the UI never blocks — INP stays low.
rubric.notes: The practical page's detail slider trades size for speed (384-1024 px) without ever blocking the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy image work off main thread web worker; dense output composite without blocking
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks; break up / offload heavy synchronous processing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference AND the per-pixel drawing+overlay composite run in a dedicated module Web Worker; the line drawing and overlay ImageBitmaps are transferred back (zero-copy). The main thread does only bounded canvas draws.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([lineBmp, overlayBmp]); UI responsive during tracing; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive two column control panel with canvas image output and before/after reveal without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing, logical properties and flexible tracks (fr/minmax) before fixed widths; content-first flexbox with flex-wrap; use min-inline-size:0 so flex children can shrink — fewer media queries, no overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — two-col is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; param-grid is grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); reveal/canvases inline-size:100% block-size:auto; the leaderboard bar uses inline-size % + min-inline-size:2px; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px horizontal overflow measured on all 5 pages at mobile 360×740 DPR3 AND desktop 1280×800

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for model loading progress; expose canvas-rendered content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvases carry role=img + aria-label (source photo / line drawing / ink overlay); the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext; every button has a name.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; canvas aria-labels present

openQuestions: The upstream carolineec/informative-drawings ships multiple styles (anime line-art, contour, opensketch); only the line-art checkpoint is exported here. Adding the contour/opensketch styles as a style selector would enrich the demo IF clean ONNX exports exist.

The ONNX re-export repo (rocca) restates no license; upstream is MIT. A canonical/first-party ONNX export with an explicit license would remove the small provenance ambiguity (disclosed honestly in at-a-glance).

followUpGoals.goal: If clean ONNX exports of the other Informative Drawings styles (contour, opensketch) exist and run on the WASM EP, add a style selector (additive, same page) so users can switch drawing styles.
Add a dedicated 'line-art as ControlNet conditioning' multi-model demo once a browser-runnable image generator that accepts a line/soft-edge conditioning image is available on-device.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for informative-drawings-lineart (rocca/informative-drawings-line-art-onnx, image-to-image line-art extraction, fp32 single-file ~17 MB — a DISTINCT vision capability not previously built: turning a photo into an artistic line drawing; the surface-normals demo already covers per-pixel orientation, so this is a substitute for the requested normals target, which was already built). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED FIRST in headless Chrome: the real ONNX graph produced genuine sparse line drawings whose ink lands on scene structure (77% paper / ~22% line on a city street; ~40% on a mountain), NOT noise or a flat field. transformers.js has no informative-drawings class, so it runs the ONNX directly via a per-worker onnxruntime-web pin. Full demo driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver: Download ~17 MB -> auto-init ready -> real output on the index + all 4 ladder pages (basics/practical line drawings + PNG download; wild ink-coverage leaderboard; multi-model chained Depth Anything v2, line-art 2666 ms + depth 3697 ms). responsive-check 0 px overflow on all 5 pages at desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Ladder: basics (trace a photo), practical (printable colouring page + download at up to 1024 px), wild (ink-coverage leaderboard across scenes), multi-model (line-art + depth = structure two ways). License: upstream carolineec/informative-drawings MIT; ONNX re-export restates none (disclosed in at-a-glance + footer).
